title: Echinospermum echinophorum
taxonomy: Echinospermum echinophorum is classified under the family Annonaceae and is native to South America. The genus Echinospermum is part of the order Magnoliales. The Annonaceae family, to which Echinospermum echinophorum belongs, is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. Within the Annonaceae family, Echinospermum echinophorum is one of the species found in South America. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the order Magnoliales.
uses: Echinospermum echinophorum, a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihelmintic properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions.
